blurb: The Parivāra, “the Compendium”, is a technical analysis of the content of the Suttavibhaṅga and the Khandhakas. It summarizes and distils the essence of the Vinaya, leaving out all narratives and stories. The Parivāra is unique to the Theravada school and it is probable that it was compiled in the sectarian period. Its manner of analysis shares certain characteristics with the Abhidhamma, such as expanding and filling in schemes of classification not given in full detail in the other canonical texts.

children.translated_name: Questions and answers on the monks’ Monastic Code and its analysis
children.blurb: Explains the origins of each Vinaya rule for monks. Includes a set of verses detailing the lineage of Vinaya teachers.

children.translated_name: The number of offenses within each rule
children.blurb: Defines the number of sub-offences that may be committed when breaking a rule.

children.translated_name: The classes of failure to which each rule belongs
children.blurb: Relates the Vinaya rules to “falling away” (vippatti) from good conduct.

children.translated_name: The classes of offenses to which each rule belongs
children.blurb: Defines which rules may fall under the heading of other rules.

children.translated_name: The number of originations of each offense
children.blurb: Defines the transgressions as originating in body, speech, or mind.

children.translated_name: The legal issues to which each offense belongs
children.blurb: Relates the Vinaya rules to the four classes of legal procedure.

children.translated_name: How each offense is settled
children.blurb: Relates the Vinaya rules to the seven kinds of legal procedure.

children.translated_name: Summary of the previous six sections
children.blurb: This concatenates the results of previous chapters.

children.translated_name: The thirteen groups of originations of offenses
children.blurb: Summarizes the rules and their origins in verse form. Begins with a set of verses on the Dhamma.

children.translated_name: Various topics with a focus on the origination of offenses
children.blurb: A complex set of permutations in applying rules for monks and nuns.

children.translated_name: The legal issues and their settling
children.blurb: Details the circumstances for applying each of the four classes of procedure.

children.translated_name: The classes of offenses in the Chapters
children.blurb: Questions on the contents of each chapter of the Khandhakas.

children.translated_name: The numerical method
children.blurb: Vinaya topics arranged by number from one to eleven, in a manner similar to the Aṅguttara Nikāya.

children.translated_name: Aspects of the legal procedures
children.blurb: Defines the beginning, middle, and end of Sangha procedures.

children.translated_name: The ten reasons for the laying down of training rules
children.blurb: Explains the ten reasons for the laying down of Vinaya rules.

children.translated_name: Verses on the training rules
children.blurb: Questions and answers on Vinaya topics in the form of an exchange of verses.

children.translated_name: Various aspects of the four kinds of legal issues
children.blurb: Analyses the seven kinds of Vinaya procedure.

children.translated_name: Verses on how to accuse properly
children.blurb: A set of verses on the topic of admonishment.

children.translated_name: Accusations and the process of investigation
children.blurb: An analysis of the circumstances considered when admonishing someone.

children.translated_name: The short chapter on the resolution of conflicts
children.blurb: The character of one who is following the Vinaya.

children.translated_name: The great chapter on the resolution of conflicts
children.blurb: Requirements when a monastic is speaking on a matter of conflict before the Sangha.

children.translated_name: The robe-making ceremony
children.blurb: Summary of the procedures pertaining to the Kathina.

children.translated_name: Ven. Upāli questions the Buddha
children.blurb: Sets of five qualities presented as a conversation between the Buddha and Venerable Upāli.

children.translated_name: The origination of offenses
children.blurb: Different mind states and origins for various rules.

children.translated_name: Verses on offenses, training rules, and legal procedures
children.blurb: Questions and answers in verse form.

children.translated_name: The sudorific verses
children.blurb: A set of Vinaya puzzles regarded as so difficult they make you sweat.

children.translated_name: Legal procedures, why the Monastic Law, resolution of legal issues
children.blurb: Vinaya topics divided into the validity of formal acts; the reasons for Vinaya; the kinds of procedure entailed; reasons for the procedures; and a nine-fold system of classification.
